answers.field_label: What Products Or Services Do You Offer
answers.value:
Riverside Roasters Coffee Co. is a small-batch specialty coffee roastery and café focused on ethically sourced beans and approachable coffee education for everyday people, not just coffee geeks. Our core offering is freshly roasted coffee beans sold both wholesale (to local cafés, offices, and small grocery stores) and retail (through our café and online store). We roast in-house 3–4 times per week and feature a rotating selection of single-origin beans (e.g., Ethiopia, Colombia, Guatemala) as well as a few signature blends that we’ve developed based on customer feedback.

In the café, we serve espresso-based drinks (lattes, cappuccinos, flat whites, cortados, americanos), brewed coffee (drip, pour-over, and French press), and a small but curated menu of teas and seasonal specialty drinks (for example, in the fall we do a house-made pumpkin spice latte using real pumpkin purée and our own spice mix, not syrup). We also offer dairy alternatives (oat, almond, and soy) and have at least one feature drink each month that we promote on social media.

Beyond beverages, we sell a limited selection of house-made and locally sourced baked goods and light snacks—things like banana bread made from my grandmother’s recipe, vegan muffins, gluten-friendly brownies from a local bakery we partner with, and small sandwiches and breakfast wraps. We intentionally keep the food menu simple so we can focus on quality and reduce waste, but we rotate items regularly and use customer feedback forms at the counter to decide what to keep.

On the product side, we sell branded merchandise such as reusable coffee cups, ceramic mugs featuring local artists, tote bags, T-shirts, and enamel pins with coffee-related designs. We also sell basic home-brewing equipment like French presses, pour-over kits, grinders, and reusable filters, and we package beginner-friendly brewing guides with each purchase so people feel confident making good coffee at home.

A growing part of our business is our coffee subscription service, where customers can sign up to receive freshly roasted beans delivered monthly or bi-weekly. We offer different grind settings depending on whether they brew with a French press, drip machine, or espresso, and we include tasting notes and origin stories with each shipment. We also work with a few local offices and co-working spaces to provide recurring office coffee services, which includes beans, equipment consultation, and occasional on-site training about how to get the best out of their machines.

Finally, we run educational workshops and small group classes—both in person and online—on topics such as: "Coffee Brewing 101 for Beginners," "Intro to Latte Art," and "How to Taste Coffee Like a Pro." These are usually scheduled on Sunday afternoons or weekday evenings. We charge per seat, and we’ve found they are a great way to build community and create more loyal, engaged customers who then buy our beans regularly. We also occasionally host private events like team-building barista workshops for local businesses and coffee-tasting nights for small groups.

answers.field_label: Who Is Your Typical Customer Or Client
answers.value:
Our typical customer or client falls into a few clear segments, but they all share a desire for better-quality coffee and a sense of community rather than a snobby, intimidating coffee culture.

1) Local neighbourhood regulars: These are people who live or work within a 2–3 km radius of our café in central Edmonton—often professionals, students, and young families. Many of them stop by on their way to work or on weekend walks along the river valley. They tend to be in the 25–45 age range, though we have older regulars as well, and they appreciate that we remember their usual order (for example, we have a customer named Raj who always gets a 12 oz oat milk flat white, and a teacher named Christine who comes every Thursday morning for a drip coffee and a muffin before school). They care about taste, but they also care about sustainability, so they like that we focus on ethically sourced beans and offer a discount if they bring a reusable cup.

2) Early-stage coffee enthusiasts and home brewers: These customers might have a basic coffee setup at home—like a French press or a simple drip machine—but they want to upgrade their daily coffee without feeling overwhelmed by technical jargon. They usually find us either through Instagram, Google Maps, or word-of-mouth from friends. They are interested in learning how grind size, water temperature, and brew time affect flavour, but they’re not necessarily trying to become baristas. Many of them sign up for our brewing workshops or ask a lot of questions at the counter when they’re choosing beans. We often see them transition from buying pre-ground coffee to buying a grinder and experimenting more at home. These customers appreciate that we provide simple, printed brew guides and are patient with questions.

3) Small local businesses and offices: This includes co-working spaces, small design agencies, local accounting firms, and a couple of non-profits who want to offer better coffee to their staff and visitors without signing up for a big corporate coffee contract. They typically order our beans on a recurring monthly or bi-weekly basis, sometimes along with basic equipment like grinders and brewers. For example, we supply coffee to a 20-person architecture firm downtown and a co-working space that hosts a lot of freelancers and start-ups. These clients value reliability (consistent deliveries and quality), transparent pricing, and the fact that they’re supporting a local, independent roaster rather than a large chain.

4) Values-driven consumers: Across all the above segments, a large portion of our customers are very conscious about where their products come from. They routinely ask about how the farmers are paid, whether the coffee is organic or Rainforest Alliance certified, and what our environmental practices are. Many of them are willing to pay a bit more for coffee if they know it is sourced responsibly and that part of the profit goes back to origin communities. They are typically well-informed, socially conscious individuals who also shop at local farmers’ markets and support other independent businesses. They love that we share origin stories on our packaging and on social media, such as details about a women-led cooperative we buy from in Guatemala.

5) Online shoppers and subscription customers: These customers often live outside our immediate neighbourhood—some are in other parts of Alberta and a few in other provinces. They might have discovered us through social media, a friend’s recommendation, or after visiting Edmonton and wanting to keep buying our coffee after they returned home. They tend to be comfortable ordering online, enjoy trying new roasts, and appreciate the convenience of having beans delivered on a schedule. Many of them are busy professionals or parents who don’t always have time to visit a café in person, but still care a lot about having good coffee at home.

Overall, our typical customer or client is someone who cares about quality, wants to understand what they’re drinking (at least at a basic level), and likes the feeling of supporting a local, independently owned business. They may not identify as “coffee experts,” but they are curious, open to learning, and responsive to personal recommendations and stories behind each coffee we feature.
